title: Dial N For Nerder

jokes.text: Come and get me, you syrupy stack of man-cakes.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently challenging food items to battle
jokes.punchline: The absurdly elaborate insult 'syrupy stack of man-cakes' for what's likely pancakes
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that's distinctly Homer-esque. The alliteration and absurd personification of breakfast food shows good craft.

jokes.text: It's too much work. Why don't you come and get me?
jokes.setup: After dramatically challenging his breakfast
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately gives up and asks the food to come to him instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness subverting his own dramatic moment. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, boy, dinner time! The perfect break between work and drunk.
jokes.setup: Homer's excitement about dinner time
jokes.punchline: Revealing his daily schedule is just work-dinner-getting drunk
jokes.explanation: Efficient dark comedy that perfectly captures Homer's priorities. The casual way he mentions his alcoholism is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Wait. What's that smell? It's like a stalk of broccoli fell in a pot of boiling water.
jokes.setup: Homer detecting an unfamiliar smell
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate way of describing the simple act of cooking vegetables
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing but a bit wordy. Shows Homer's unfamiliarity with healthy food preparation.

jokes.text: But I have all those old diets I still haven't finished.
jokes.setup: Marge introducing a new diet
jokes.punchline: Homer treating diets like unfinished homework or projects
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of how people think about diets. The idea of 'finishing' a diet like homework is brilliantly absurd and very Homer.

jokes.text: I'll bet no man would touch you.
jokes.setup: Nutritionist saying she used to weigh 400 pounds
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt, tactless response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer tactlessness. Not particularly clever but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Bell pepper repetition sequence - Instead of French toast... bell pepper, craving sugar... bell pepper, want beer... bell pepper
jokes.setup: Nutritionist explaining diet substitutions
jokes.punchline: Bell pepper as the absurd answer to every craving
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and repetition for comedic effect. Sets up the running gag effectively.

jokes.text: It's tangy like a pepper, but crunchy like a bell!
jokes.setup: Homer trying bell peppers for the first time
jokes.punchline: His backwards description that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Homer's confused logic about the pepper's name vs. its qualities is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Bell peppers... piping cold, crunchy and bland.
jokes.setup: Street vendor selling hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Instead selling bell peppers with intentionally unappealing description
jokes.explanation: Good callback that parodies street vendor calls while emphasizing how unappealing the diet substitute is.

jokes.text: Three, please.
jokes.setup: Vendor selling bland bell peppers
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically buying multiple despite the unappealing description
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Homer's enthusiasm for the terrible diet food continues the gag.

jokes.text: Well, they say muscle weighs more than fat. / Homer, your body is all fat.
jokes.setup: Homer making excuse about weight gain
jokes.punchline: Marge's blunt factual correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure with Marge's deadpan delivery cutting through Homer's excuse.

jokes.text: I'm not even gonna dignify that with eye contact.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if he's been cheating on his diet
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top offended response while clearly guilty
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's guilty overreaction and dramatic flair.

jokes.text: I'm looking for something, too... a bell pepper I don't like. Maybe it'll be this one. Wrong again.
jokes.setup: Marge searching for evidence of diet cheating
jokes.punchline: Homer pretending to look for a bell pepper he doesn't like while clearly enjoying them
jokes.explanation: Clever callback showing Homer's transparent attempt to cover his tracks while indulging.

jokes.text: See that fat lady with the moustache? That's you. See that hippo rolling in dung? You're the dung.
jokes.setup: Sibling insult exchange
jokes.punchline: Lisa's comeback is more elaborate and cutting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in sibling rivalry with Lisa's more sophisticated insult.

jokes.text: Oh, but hers was better!
jokes.setup: Homer stopping the kids' argument after each got one insult
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting Lisa's insult was superior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's grudging respect for Lisa's wit.

jokes.text: He used to be so loving. Now he says he's always working late, but when he comes home, his needles aren't even bloody!
jokes.setup: Woman suspecting her acupuncturist boyfriend of cheating
jokes.punchline: Her evidence is that his acupuncture needles aren't bloody from real work
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of typical cheating signs with profession-specific 'evidence.'

jokes.text: Most cheating happens when you think everything is just great.
jokes.setup: TV show about catching cheaters
jokes.punchline: Cynical advice that you should be suspicious even when happy
jokes.explanation: Dark but realistic commentary on paranoia and relationships.

jokes.text: Out of the house, kids! I have to make an adult phone call.
jokes.setup: Marge preparing to call the cheating show
jokes.punchline: Her treating it like something sexual rather than just embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's embarrassment about the situation.

jokes.text: Oh, man, not again... How would you...? Come on.
jokes.setup: Kids being kicked out for Marge's 'adult phone call'
jokes.punchline: Kids thinking it's something sexual happening regularly
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the misunderstanding but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Springfield's National Park - Now smaller than ever
jokes.setup: Park entrance sign
jokes.punchline: Advertising the park's diminishing size as if it's a selling point
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons sight gag satirizing bureaucratic incompetence and environmental destruction.

jokes.text: The Internet wrote it. I just handed it in.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning Bart wrote a report on the park
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual admission of plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Timely commentary on internet plagiarism delivered with Bart's characteristic lack of shame.

jokes.text: Cool! The strong kicking the weak's butt. / It's disgusting!
jokes.setup: Watching animals fight
jokes.punchline: Bart and Lisa's opposite reactions perfectly matching their characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast in worldviews with efficient execution

jokes.text: I didn't used to mind it when he'd lock the bathroom door and snack off, but when he's getting it regular, night after night after night...
jokes.setup: Marge on TV explaining Homer's diet cheating
jokes.punchline: Her language making it sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that works on both levels - diet cheating and sexual innuendo. Very cringe-worthy in the best way.

jokes.text: Dude, it's Saturday. What kind of a freak wastes his...? / May I join you?
jokes.setup: Bart mocking Martin for archaeology on Saturday
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately wanting to join the 'freaky' activity
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Lisa's nerdiness vs. Bart's attitude, with nice comic timing.

jokes.text: You can brush, and I can blow! / Well, I agree you blow. / A lot of people blow, but no one blows like you.
jokes.setup: Martin innocently talking about archaeology techniques
jokes.punchline: Bart and Martin unknowingly creating sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Juvenile double entendre that works because of Martin's innocence and Bart's obliviousness.

jokes.text: When you look up blow in the dictionary... / Bart, he's not gonna get it!
jokes.setup: Bart continuing the innuendo
jokes.punchline: Lisa breaking the fourth wall to explain Martin won't understand
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment with Lisa as the voice of reason, acknowledging the joke structure.

jokes.text: The bone's the very thing, methinks, to prank my sister and the dink.
jokes.setup: Bart finding a bone for pranking
jokes.punchline: His mock-Shakespearean soliloquy about pranking
jokes.explanation: Clever elevated language for a simple prank, showing off Bart's unexpected sophistication when plotting mischief.

jokes.text: It could be one of the major homos! / You're one of the major...
jokes.setup: Martin getting excited about human bone discovery
jokes.punchline: Scientific term 'homo sapiens' allowing for juvenile misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Fairly obvious juvenile wordplay that relies on scientific terminology. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I can bike away much faster, and ring the bell to drown out bad thoughts!
jokes.setup: Bart running from the accident scene
jokes.punchline: His childish coping mechanism of drowning out guilt with bike bells
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Bart's childish psychology in dealing with serious consequences. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Let's watch TV! / Happy, happy safe at home
jokes.setup: Kids trying to cope with Martin's presumed death
jokes.punchline: TV's innocent children's programming contrasting with their guilt
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony with the innocent TV show contrasting their guilt and fear.

jokes.text: Sadly, his pocket was protected, but nothing else.
jokes.setup: News report about Martin's pocket protector being found
jokes.punchline: Morbid pun on pocket protector's name vs. what it actually protected
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark wordplay that's both morbid and clever. Perfect news reporter gallows humor.

jokes.text: Chief, that's evidence. / I know, but after it's evidence, it's a shirt again, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Chief wanting to take Martin's shirt for his son
jokes.punchline: His twisted logic about evidence eventually becoming regular items again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum logic - absurd but delivered with complete confidence. Shows his unique brand of incompetent reasoning.

jokes.text: Hmm? Oh, dead. Definitely dead.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking for final word on Martin's condition
jokes.punchline: Chief's casual, distracted confirmation of death
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery showing Chief's casual attitude toward serious matters.

jokes.text: Hey, I was trying to help him. At worst, I'm an accomplice.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling them murderers
jokes.punchline: Bart's attempt to minimize his guilt with legal technicalities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart trying to rationalize his guilt with kid logic.

jokes.text: Got any Joyce Carol Oates? / Nope. It's all Danielle Steel.
jokes.setup: Lisa seeking literary fiction from bookmobile
jokes.punchline: Only having romance novels available
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's sophistication vs. reality of available resources.

jokes.text: I'm saying this is worse than a million A minuses!
jokes.setup: Lisa considering not telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Her academic-focused scale for measuring moral catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - using academic achievement as her scale for moral disaster is brilliantly in character.

jokes.text: We were never outside. We were here all day. / That quick talking is never a good sign.
jokes.setup: Kids nervously providing alibi
jokes.punchline: Marge's parental wisdom about suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Good parental observation that rings true - nervous over-explaining is always suspicious.

jokes.text: I, uh,... have to count the atoms at the nuclear plant. Conservation of mass. It's the law.
jokes.setup: Homer making excuse to leave on Saturday
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-scientific explanation for unnecessary work
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - using half-understood science to justify his actions. The absurdity of counting atoms is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: It'll finally win me the respect of my father, the Reverend Billy Graham, Junior.
jokes.setup: TV producer talking about his show
jokes.punchline: Revealing his father is Billy Graham Jr. (implying he's Billy Graham III)
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the contrast between sleazy TV and religious legacy.

jokes.text: Martin Priss, Martin Princess, Fartin' Prince, Martin the Brown-Nosed Reindeer
jokes.setup: Listing Martin's nicknames at his memorial
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and mean variations on his name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of playground cruelty, though fairly predictable wordplay.

jokes.text: Martin was a shy, awkward child who will never fulfill his promise to become a weird, unloved adult.
jokes.setup: Memorial eulogy for Martin
jokes.punchline: Backhanded tribute predicting his adult loneliness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark eulogy that's both mean and oddly touching. The structure of 'promising' to become unloved is masterful.

jokes.text: Usually when I do something bad, I feel good. But not this time. Martin was like Jesus, only real.
jokes.setup: Jimbo expressing guilt over Martin's death
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of Jesus while elevating Martin
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Jimbo's twisted moral compass and casual blasphemy while showing genuine grief.

jokes.text: Martin was afraid of heights. What's he doing on a cliff? It don't add up. / Hey, look who's trying to add.
jokes.setup: Nelson showing unexpected detective skills
jokes.punchline: Jimbo mocking him for using math/logic
jokes.explanation: Great character interplay showing both Nelson's hidden intelligence and Jimbo's anti-intellectual bias.

jokes.text: I used to make documentaries about coal miners, migrant workers, things that mattered. / Yeah yeah, we're all whores. Just get in there.
jokes.setup: Cameraman lamenting his career decline
jokes.punchline: Producer's cynical dismissal of artistic integrity
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on media degradation with brutally honest dialogue about selling out.

jokes.text: What do you mean 'what's up'? Why would something be up? Yeah, nothing's up, nothing's down, except for Martin, who died.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what's up with the kids
jokes.punchline: Their nervous over-explanation ending with blurting out Martin's death
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how guilt makes you reveal exactly what you're trying to hide.

jokes.text: Well, I certainly wasn't eating chips and salsa from this briefcase.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to hide his diet cheating
jokes.punchline: His transparent denial while clearly doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer denial while obviously guilty. Good parallel to the kids' suspicious behavior.

jokes.text: Hey, you little murderer. / What?! / Those shades are killing me, they're so cute.
jokes.setup: Calling Bart a murderer
jokes.punchline: Revealing he means the sunglasses are 'killing' him metaphorically
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that plays on Bart's guilt while being completely innocent.

jokes.text: We can't be seen together. / But we're brother and sister. / Exactly.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying they can't be seen together
jokes.punchline: Her logic that being siblings makes it more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid logic showing how guilt makes normal relationships seem suspicious.

jokes.text: I like how 'Kwik' is spelled with a 'K.' It's a quicker way of spelling 'quick.'
jokes.setup: Chief making small talk about the store name
jokes.punchline: His backward logic about the spelling being 'quicker'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum logic - confidently wrong in a completely unique way that only he would think.

jokes.text: Found this bone tied to a string. Almost like someone was playing a prank on him.
jokes.setup: Chief investigating Martin's death
jokes.punchline: His accidentally correct deduction about the prank
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Wiggum accidentally getting close to the truth despite his usual incompetence.

jokes.text: Everyone likes pranks, e-even Dr. Hibbert. / I hate pranks.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming everyone likes pranks
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction from the person he just cited
jokes.explanation: Good timing with immediate contradiction of Bart's claim

jokes.text: I can't live with the guilt. I'm going to Martin's house to end it all
jokes.setup: Bart's dramatic note suggesting suicide
jokes.punchline: Later revealed he meant ending Martin's butterfly project
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection that plays on dramatic suicide note tropes before revealing innocent meaning.

jokes.text: By which I meant bring Martin's butterfly project to completion.
jokes.setup: Previous ominous note about 'ending it all'
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation about finishing Martin's science project
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the misdirection showing Bart's good intentions despite dramatic language.

jokes.text: I'm on a freight train to Crazy Town.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing his mental state under stress
jokes.punchline: His melodramatic metaphor for losing his mind
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's dramatic flair even when genuinely distressed.

jokes.text: Congratulations on purchasing the Sanyo DL30, the finest non-reversing tape player
jokes.setup: Martin's tape seeming to come back on mysteriously
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's just the tape player's instruction message
jokes.explanation: Good technical humor explaining the 'supernatural' tape phenomenon with mundane reality.

jokes.text: Clawdy-claw, clickety-cloo, clawdy-claw...
jokes.setup: Martin's voice on tape calling to butterflies
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up butterfly language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin nerdiness with absurd scientific confidence

jokes.text: Pudding power, activate!
jokes.setup: Homer being confronted about his diet cheating
jokes.punchline: His superhero-style battle cry for dessert
jokes.explanation: Good parody of superhero tropes applied to Homer's food obsessions.

jokes.text: I'd rather have a chubby hubby than a sexy exy.
jokes.setup: TV producer trying to break up the marriage
jokes.punchline: Marge's rhyming declaration of loyalty
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with cute rhyming that shows Marge's loyalty and values.

jokes.text: If there was a reality show called Fat Guys Who Really Love Their Wives, not only would it be an enormous ratings success, but I'd be the first one on it.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Marge's loyalty
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and self-aware TV show concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor with Homer perfectly understanding his demographic appeal while being genuinely sweet.

jokes.text: I'm going to the editing room to make you look insane.
jokes.setup: Producer being rejected by Marge
jokes.punchline: His honest admission of manipulative editing practices
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of reality TV manipulation with brutal honesty about media manipulation.

jokes.text: I almost flicked an innocent butterfly.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa fighting in Martin's butterfly room
jokes.punchline: Bart's concern about collateral damage to insects while ignoring human consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bart's twisted priorities - concerned about butterflies but not human consequences.

jokes.text: Listen, world, we killed Martin Prince!
jokes.setup: Lisa deciding to confess
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic confession to no one in particular
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's dramatic guilt and need to confess.

jokes.text: You almost got away with it, but here's where you got sloppy: shouting out that you did it.
jokes.setup: Nelson arresting them for Martin's murder
jokes.punchline: His detective analysis of their obvious mistake
jokes.explanation: Good detective parody with Nelson's matter-of-fact analysis of their amateur mistake.

jokes.text: I'll let you cherry-bomb my Malibu Stacy.
jokes.setup: Lisa desperately trying to bribe Nelson
jokes.punchline: Her offering to let him destroy her prized possession
jokes.explanation: Good character desperation showing Lisa willing to sacrifice what matters most to her.

jokes.text: To quote Mark Twain, 'rumors of my death have been greatly exaggerated.'
jokes.setup: Martin revealing he's alive
jokes.punchline: His appropriate literary quote for the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin moment - using literary quotes appropriately even in dramatic situations.

jokes.text: For years I've worn underwear with a wedgie-accommodating waistband. On that fateful day, these mighty whiteys saved my life.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he survived the fall
jokes.punchline: His special underwear designed for bullying saved him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Martin's nerdy preparation for bullying accidentally becomes heroic. 'Mighty whiteys' is perfect wordplay.

jokes.text: I spent an hour fashioning an escape raft and three days crafting this modesty skirt.
jokes.setup: Martin explaining his survival activities
jokes.punchline: He spent three times longer on modesty than escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin priorities - more concerned with propriety than survival. The time contrast is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: I learned that beneath my goody two-shoes lie some very dark socks.
jokes.setup: Lisa reflecting on the experience
jokes.punchline: Her metaphorical description of discovering her dark side
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa metaphor connecting clothing to morality. The 'dark socks' image is both poetic and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And I learned that killing a nerd is not as fun as it sounds.
jokes.setup: Bart sharing his lesson learned
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that he expected murder to be enjoyable
jokes.explanation: Darkly perfect Bart logic - disappointed that murder wasn't as entertaining as anticipated. Brutally efficient and in character.

tmdb_overview: Homer cheats on his diet, so Marge gets a reality show called "The Sneakers" to check up on him. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa fear that a prank they played on Martin Prince had deadly consequences.
